Name: mixed_precision_hardware_optimizations
Title: Optimizing Mixed-Precision Quantization Through Hardware-Driven Kernel Design
Short Hypothesis: By developing a system that optimally aligns mixed-precision quantization strategies with hardware-level kernel optimizations, we can achieve unprecedented latency reductions without sacrificing accuracy on commodity GPUs.
Related Work: Existing works like SNIP and AMP-ViT focus on quantization strategies but do not sufficiently link these strategies to specific hardware optimizations. This proposal distinguishes itself by directly profiling the impact of mixed-precision strategies on kernel performance in a hardware-first manner, ensuring practical applicability and measurable improvements.
Abstract: The rapid increase in model complexity necessitates efficient execution of deep learning workloads on commodity hardware. This proposal introduces a novel framework that synergizes mixed-precision quantization techniques with hardware-level optimizations for GPU kernels. The core of this approach lies in systematically profiling existing kernels and quantization strategies to identify optimal configurations that minimize latency while maintaining accuracy. By employing detailed performance metrics such as achieved bandwidth, occupancy, and arithmetic intensity, we will iteratively refine our quantization approach, ultimately leading to a robust, optimized inference pipeline. Initial experiments will focus on well-known architectures, leveraging existing benchmarks to ensure reproducibility. Expected outcomes include significant speedups in inference performance on commodity GPUs, validated through rigorous profiling and comparison with state-of-the-art techniques. This work will lay the groundwork for future explorations in hardware-software co-design specific to mixed-precision quantization.
Experiments: Benchmark existing mixed-precision quantization methods on a standard dataset using a commodity GPU.
Implement a profiling tool to measure kernel performance across various quantization strategies, focusing on achieved bandwidth, occupancy, and latency.
Conduct A/B tests comparing the performance of the optimized quantization framework against traditional methods, measuring p50/p99 latency and throughput under batching.
Explore different layer configurations and quantization strategies to determine the most effective combinations for specific hardware setups.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the variability of hardware performance across different GPU models, which may affect the generalizability of results. Additionally, the optimization process may introduce complexity in model deployment. Limitations may also arise from the need for extensive profiling, which could limit scalability.
